schema_notes: Per-billionaire record following larry_ellison.v3.json reference. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Rennert is a low-profile, privately-held-industrial billionaire; observable giving is dominated by named-chair and institutional gifts (mostly Jewish/Israel-focused) with NO identified private foundation filing a 990-PF under his name. Absence of a 990-PF vehicle is itself the central finding for tier assignment.

person.wealth_source: The Renco Group (private industrial holding company); subsidiaries include AM General (military truckmaker — Humvee), Doe Run Company (lead/zinc), U.S. Magnesium Corp., former WCI Steel. Wealth built via junk-bond financed acquisitions in the 1980s-90s.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 profile places Rennert at #1137 with net worth figure not directly quoted in retrievable snippet but consistent with Nov 2024 Forbes estimate of $3.8B and celebritynetworth $4B. Historical peak ~$5.9B per Forbes 2011-2017 (when commodity prices and AM General/Humvee contracts were stronger). Range reflects uncertainty from Renco being wholly private — no public equity mark-to-market. Court-ordered $213.2M judgment (2015, affirmed 2017) trimmed liquid wealth; $8M+ unpaid Baltimore water bills (Sparrows Point) and ongoing environmental liabilities at Doe Run and US Magnesium further cloud the figure.

sources.value_note: Profile confirms #1137 rank on 2026 list. Snippet text highlights 2015 court ruling and Western Wall donation. Precise NW figure not quoted in retrievable snippet; inferred $3.8B consistent with Nov 2024.
Profile exists; daily figure not captured in snippet. Consistent with $3.5-4.0B range.

sources.publisher: Forbes (2011 Billionaire Houses feature — historical peak)

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Concentrated in Renco Group — wholly private, no public float, primary value in cyclical commodity businesses (lead, magnesium) and defense (AM General Humvee). Private industrial/commodity wealth is among the LEAST liquid billionaire asset classes: no share sales, no dividend yield transparency, significant environmental remediation liabilities sitting inside operating subs. Additionally: $213.2M court judgment (affirmed 2017) against Rennert personally for improper fund diversion reduces liquid wealth. Apply 15% liquidity for giving-capacity-now calculations — lower than Ellison's 25% because Ellison has liquid Tesla + Oracle stock; Rennert has none.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1. Rennert is a longtime Republican donor — McCain, Giuliani, Trump.
political_giving.observed_total_note: March 12, 2020: Rennert and wife each gave $450,000 to Trump Victory ($900K combined), described by Forbes as the largest individual contributions ever made in their names. Earlier (through ~2011): $500K+ over five years to various GOP committees per ABC News. 2022 cycle: $2,900 to Josh Hawley (Forbes Jan 2022). Lifetime political giving likely $2-5M range; direct FEC bulk pull not yet run.

detected_vehicles.foundations_active_note: CRITICAL FINDING: No private foundation bearing 'Ira Rennert', 'Ingeborg Rennert', 'Rennert Family' or 'Renco' is indexed in ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer (searched April 2026). This is highly unusual for a ~$4B billionaire with 30+ years of giving history. Multiple hypotheses: (1) giving flows through direct checks to existing institutions rather than a grantmaking vehicle; (2) giving flows through a DAF at Jewish Communal Fund or similar (donor-opaque); (3) giving flows through a foundation with a name not obviously connected to Rennert (searched 'Rennert' variants, 'Renco', 'Fair Field' with no hits); (4) wealth is held in trust structures that give directly without intermediate 990-PF filings. Absence of 990-PF means annual giving is NOT publicly auditable.
detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No direct DAF evidence. High likelihood of DAF usage given (a) Orthodox Jewish community norms favoring Jewish Communal Fund / Jewish Federations DAFs, (b) absence of private foundation, (c) pattern of named-institutional gifts that could easily be disbursed from a DAF. Could not be verified from available sources.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC identified. Renco Group itself is Rennert's for-profit holding company, not a philanthropic vehicle.

(operations, not wealth vehicle). 2015 court ruling found Rennert diverted funds from his business to fund Fair Field mansion — the diversion vehicle was Renco-controlled 'Blue Turtles LLC'. Not offshore.

non_philanthropic_assets_for_context.name: Fair Field (Sagaponack, NY)
non_philanthropic_assets_for_context.amenities: Basketball court, bowling alley, two tennis courts, two squash courts, carousel, swimming pools, $150K hot tub
non_philanthropic_assets_for_context.note: Largest private residence in the United States. Explicitly NOT philanthropy. Listed here because the scale of personal-consumption asset investment is directly relevant to the giving-gap calculation — at minimum $198M in a single residence vs. ~$13M in documented lifetime giving with disclosed dollar figures.

red_flags.evidence: Rennert is NOT on the Giving Pledge list as of April 2026. At 30+ years as a billionaire and age 91, no public majority-of-wealth commitment has ever been made.

ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer returns zero private foundations with 'Rennert' in the name (searched April 2026). For a ~$4B billionaire, absence of a 501(c)(3) grantmaking vehicle means ANNUAL GIVING TOTALS ARE NOT PUBLICLY AUDITABLE. Observable giving is limited to named-chair/named-building gifts whose exact dollar figures are mostly not disclosed.

Rennert's Renco subsidiaries have been at the center of major environmental-health litigation: (1) DOJ sued US Magnesium for ~$1B in toxic waste fines (2001, settled in Renco's favor 2007); (2) Doe Run Missouri lead smelter in Herculaneum operated with severe lead-poisoning impacts until closure in 2013; (3) Doe Run Peru (La Oroya) placed on Blacksmith Institute's ten most polluted global sites list, unable to meet Peruvian environmental standards. None of Rennert's documented philanthropy has gone to children of Peru, Herculaneum, or otherwise remediated communities harmed by Renco operations. Philanthropic spending is orders of magnitude smaller than cumulative environmental externalities.

$900K single-day contribution to Trump Victory in March 2020 (described by Forbes as largest individual contribution ever in their names) — this ONE political event exceeds all individually-disclosed-dollar charity gifts EXCEPT the Jerusalem settlers ($25.4M, unverified source) and Wiesel Center ($5M, decades-old). Political-to-charitable substitution signal is strong.

Fair Field, the 110,000 sqft Sagaponack mansion, is valued between $198M and $500M — the single largest private residence in the United States. Ira Rennert reportedly spent more on one house than on all documented, dollar-amount-disclosed philanthropic gifts combined (~$13M with disclosed amounts, plus $25M unverified for settler orgs).

2015 federal court ruling (affirmed 2017 by 2nd Circuit) found Rennert liable for $213.2M for improperly diverting money from his business (Magnesium Corp. of America) to fund Fair Field mansion. This directly evidences a pattern of prioritizing personal-consumption asset accumulation over fiduciary (and by extension charitable) use of capital.

DAF_TRANSFERS — Unverified; no foundation to trace transfers FROM

rollup.observable_giving_method: Sum of gifts WITH disclosed dollar figures: Wiesel Center $5M + Lincoln Center $0.25M + WTC Memorial $1M + YU/Sy Syms $1M + Barnard $2.5M + Center for Jewish History $1M + NYU chair $2M (estimated). Excludes: Jerusalem settlers $25.4M (unverified source); all gifts with naming rights but no public dollar figure (Western Wall Hall of Light, Columbia Rennert Hall, Fifth Ave Mikvah, Bar-Ilan Rennert Center, Yeshivat Netiv Aryeh); political giving; all religious/Israel giving without dollar disclosure.

rollup.observable_giving_range_note: Low end = only dollar-disclosed gifts. High end = adds $25.4M Jerusalem settlers (if verified) plus $10M implied for named buildings/centers without disclosed figures. Even the HIGH END is remarkably low for a 30-year billionaire — roughly 1% of net worth spread across lifetime.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.15 × $3.8B = $570M; tenure cap saturated (30 years). Expected = $57M. This uses the strict 10% norm at low liquidity. Relaxing liquidity to 25% would raise expected to $95M.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: Given (a) absence of private foundation, (b) strong Orthodox Jewish philanthropic network (Jewish Communal Fund is largest Jewish DAF), (c) clear pattern of institutional gifts to Jewish causes that could be disbursed from DAF, we set an upper bound of $75M over 30 years (~$2.5M/yr average). This is an UPPER bound, not an estimate. Could be zero.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked. Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Rennert demonstrably prefers low-profile giving (few press releases, few dollar disclosures). Apply 0.1% of liquid wealth × tenure: 0.001 × $570M × 30 = $17M. Rounded to $15M conservative.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Orthodox Jewish tithing norm ('maaser kesafim') is 10% of income; 'chomesh' (ideal) is 20%. Rennert is demonstrably observant (built Fifth Ave Synagogue mikvah, major seminary donor). At 2% of liquid wealth × 30 years = 0.02 × $570M × 30 / 30 applied once = $11.4M floor; extend to $60M as upper bound to capture decades of seminary/synagogue/Torah scroll giving that would not appear in 990-PF data even if one existed.
hidden_upper_usd.settler_seminary_method: The Bloomberg-attributed $25.4M to Jerusalem settler organizations is in the hidden-upper rather than observable because original source could not be directly verified; carried at full nominal value here as an upper bound.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires observable giving is LOW in absolute terms AND there is no credible pathway to large hidden giving that would flip the classification. Rennert qualifies because: (1) observable giving ~$13M disclosed is TINY relative to $3.8B net worth (0.3%); (2) NO Giving Pledge commitment; (3) NO private foundation; (4) documented pattern of personal-consumption asset accumulation ($250M+ mansion, $213M diverted funds judgment); (5) even with the most generous hidden-upper estimates (DAF + religious + anonymous + settler = $175M), total is 4.6% of net worth — still low. Unlike Ellison (Tier B because of disclosed trust), Rennert has NOT made any public commitment that could later be credited. Note: observable+hidden ratio to a STRICT expected (10% × 15% liquidity × 30yr = $57M) exceeds 1.0x ONLY because of the low liquidity denominator; at 25% liquidity (arguably correct for a 30-year holder who could have refinanced equity out) the ratio drops to 1.85x. Either way, absolute-dollar observable is low and no pathway to Tier B upgrade.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Rennert is a canonical example of 'old private-industrial billionaire, religious-directed giving, no foundation, no pledge, no public philanthropic identity.' This is distinct from dramatic failures-to-give like Ellison — Rennert has quietly given to Jewish causes for decades, probably more than observable, but the scale is an order of magnitude below what a $4B fortune held for 30 years would suggest under any reasonable expected-giving norm.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: v2 formula continuity only. Pledge-breach zero because no pledge was signed (signing and failing > never signing in penalty terms). Giving gap scaled by observable/expected = 0.224; tenure saturated; red flags maxed (6 flags > 4). DO NOT PUBLISH.

summary_150_words: Ira Rennert, 91, chairs Renco Group — a wholly private industrial conglomerate (AM General Humvees, Doe Run lead, US Magnesium) worth roughly $3.8B on Forbes' 2026 list (#1137). Over 30 years as a billionaire he has built NO private foundation, signed NO Giving Pledge, and left NO publicly auditable annual giving record. Observable lifetime giving with disclosed dollar amounts totals only ~$13M — dominated by Jewish and Israel-focused named chairs and buildings (BU Wiesel Center $5M, Barnard Judaic Studies $2.5M, Yeshiva Univ. Entrepreneurial Institute, Bar-Ilan Jerusalem Center, Western Wall Hall of Light, Fifth Avenue Synagogue mikvah). Unverified Bloomberg reporting adds $25.4M to Jerusalem settler orgs. Meanwhile he built Fair Field — America's largest private residence ($198-500M) — and was ordered in 2017 to repay $213M for diverting business funds to it. Tier A Verified Low: low-profile religious philanthropy that, even at generous hidden-upper estimates, falls well short of any reasonable expected-giving norm.
